claim: Hermes has self-reinforced learning loop whereas OpenClaw requires manual recursive learning setup
actionable: Use Hermes for tasks where agent improvement over time matters, OpenClaw for task orchestration

claim: Hermes uses Hancho for memory management and automatically persists learning across sessions
actionable: Leverage Hermes's built-in memory for tasks requiring learning and preference tracking

claim: 36% of OpenClaw skills on hub have known vulnerabilities versus Hermes's curated skill approval
actionable: Use Hermes for security-critical tasks or maintain strict review of OpenClaw skills

claim: Hermes and OpenClaw can work together with Hermes as brain and OpenClaw as arms
actionable: Architecture agents using Hermes for learning/reasoning and OpenClaw for execution
